title: Unified definition of ferroelectricity
authors: Wei Luo, Shihan Deng, Hongjun Xiang, Laurent Bellaiche
abstract: Recent theoretical and experimental advances in quantum ferroelectrics suggest that ferroelectricity can also emerge in non-polar space group, highlighting the limitations of conventional polar space group criteria in identifying ferroelectric materials. Here, we introduce a unified definition based on switchable polarization differences between energetically equivalent states, which naturally encompasses conventional and quantum ferroelectrics. Guided by this principle, we implement a high-throughput screening strategy that systematically identifies both conventional and quantum ferroelectrics among experimentally synthesized materials. In particular, we identify a new type of quantum ferroelectric in which the quantized polarization arises from arbitrary ionic displacements, in contrast to previous quantum ferroelectrics (including both fractional and integer quantum ferroelectrics) where quantized polarization results from fractional or integer ionic displacements. Notably, we find that materials such as Ba3I6 and Cs2PdC2 exhibit low switching barriers and robust insulating behavior, highlighting their experimental viability. Our results reconcile conventional and quantum ferroelectrics, expand the accessible materials landscape, and provide a practical roadmap for discovering next-generation ferroelectrics with advanced switchable functionalities.
summary: This paper introduces a new, unified definition of ferroelectricity that bridges the gap between classical polar materials and emerging quantum ferroelectrics. By focusing on switchable polarization differences rather than space group symmetry, the authors successfully identified dozens of new ferroelectric candidates. This work expands the search space for next-generation functional materials with controllable polarization.
main_problem: The existing definition of ferroelectricity is limited to polar space groups, failing to account for emerging quantum ferroelectrics (QFEs) that exhibit switchable polarization in non-polar space groups.
main_result: The authors propose a unified definition of ferroelectricity based on switchable polarization differences between energetically equivalent states, identifying a new class of QFEs and a large library of potential materials.
method: A high-throughput screening workflow using the Materials Project database, combined with DFT, Berry-phase calculations, and machine-learning potentials (DREAM model) to evaluate energy barriers and polarization.
model_or_system: The study covers a broad range of materials, including conventional ferroelectrics and quantum ferroelectrics (both integer and fractional), with specific focus on candidates like Ba3I6 and Cs2PdC2.
key_observables: Polarization difference (delta P), switching energy barriers, band gap, and coercive field.
important_parameters: Energy barrier threshold of 400 meV per formula unit, band gap > 0.1 eV, and quantized vs. arbitrary ionic displacements.
main_assumptions: The screening assumes that materials with low switching barriers and robust insulating behavior are experimentally viable.
figures_summary: Fig 1 shows the comparison between conventional and QFE mechanisms and the new unified framework; Fig 2 illustrates the structural construction and screening workflow; Fig 3 displays crystal structures and migration pathways for Ba3I6 and Cs2PdC2; Fig 4 shows the temperature dependence of the coercive field.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the conceptual problem of the definition gap, proposes a new unified framework, describes a high-throughput screening methodology, presents the discovery of new QFE classes and specific material candidates, and concludes with the experimental implications.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
